obj.content: What made David leave London and move to Northsea?
obj.html_content: What made David leave London and move to Northsea?

obj.html_content: what equipment it will be necessary to take.

obj.html_content: gives talks on safety at sea.

sentence.content: I never really planned to be a lifeboat volunteer when I came to live in Northsea.
sentence.content_zh: 当我来到北海生活时，我从未想过要成为一名救生艇志愿者。

sentence.content: I’d been  working in London as a website designer, but although that was interesting, I didn’t like city life.
sentence.content_zh: 我之前一直在伦敦做网站设计师，虽然工作很有趣，但我不喜欢城市生活。

sentence.content: I’d been really keen on boats as a teenager, and I thought if I went to live by the sea, I might be able to pursue that interest a bit more in my free time.
sentence.content_zh: 我年少时就非常喜欢船，我想如果我住在海边，也许在空闲时间我能更多地追求这一兴趣。

sentence.content: Then I found that the Lifeboat Institution was looking for volunteers, so I decided to apply.
sentence.content_zh: 后来我发现救生艇协会正在招募志愿者，于是我决定去申请。

sentence.content: The Lifeboat Institution building here in Northsea’s hard to miss, it’s one of the largest in the country.
sentence.content_zh: 北海的救生艇协会大楼很显眼，它是全国最大的救生艇协会之一。

sentence.content: It was built 15 years ago with funds provided by a generous member of the public who’d lived here all her life.
sentence.content_zh: 它是 15 年前由一位一生都住在这里的慷慨市民出资建造的。

sentence.content: As the Lifeboat Institution is a charity that relies on that kind of donation, rather than funding provided by the government, that was a huge help to us.
sentence.content_zh: 由于救生艇协会是一个依靠此类捐赠而非政府资金的慈善机构，这对我们来说是一个巨大的帮助。

sentence.content: When I applied, I had to have a health assessment. The doctors were particularly interested in my vision.
sentence.content_zh: 申请时，我必须接受健康评估。医生们对我的视力特别感兴趣。

sentence.content: I used to be short-sighted, so I’d had to wear glasses, but I’d had laser eye surgery two years earlier so that was OK.
sentence.content_zh: 我以前是近视眼，所以不得不戴眼镜，但两年前我做了激光眼科手术，所以视力方面没有问题。

sentence.content: They gave me tests for colour blindness and they thought I might have a problem there, but it turned out I was OK.
sentence.content_zh: 他们给我做了色盲测试并认为我可能在这方面有问题，但结果证明我没问题。

sentence.content: When the coastguard gets an alert, all the volunteers are contacted and rush to the lifeboat station.
sentence.content_zh: 当海岸警卫队收到警报时，所有志愿者都会收到通知并赶往救生艇站。

sentence.content: Our target’s to get there in five minutes, then we try to get the boat off the dock and out to sea in another six to eight minutes.
sentence.content_zh: 我们的目标是在五分钟内赶到那里，然后在六到八分钟内将船驶离码头并驶向大海。

sentence.content: Our team’s proud that we usually achieve that – the average time across the country’s eight and a half minutes.
sentence.content_zh: 我们团队引以为豪的是，我们通常都能做到这一点-全国的平均时间是八分半钟。

sentence.content: I’ve recently qualified as what’s called a ‘helmsman’, which means I have the ultimate responsibility for the lifeboat.
sentence.content_zh: 我最近获得了所谓的 "舵手 "资格，这意味着我对救生艇负有最终责任。

sentence.content: I have to check that the equipment we use is in working order – the crew have special life jackets that can support up to four people in the water.
sentence.content_zh: 我必须检查确定我们使用的设备处于正常工作状态-即船员们都有特殊的救生衣，这种救生衣在水中最多可以支撑四个人。

sentence.content: And it’s ultimately my decision whether it’s safe to launch the boat.
sentence.content_zh: 救生艇下水是否安全最终将由我做出判定。

sentence.content: But it’s very rare not to launch it, even in the worst weather.
sentence.content_zh: 但即使在最恶劣的天气下，也很少有不下水的情况。

sentence.content: As well as going out on the lifeboat, my work involves other things too.
sentence.content_zh: 除了出海救生，我的工作还包括其他方面。

sentence.content: A lot of people underestimate how quickly conditions can change at sea, so I speak to youth groups and sailing clubs in the area about the sorts of problems that sailors and swimmers can have if the weather suddenly gets bad.
sentence.content_zh: 很多人低估了海上条件变化的速度。所以我向该地区的青年团体和帆船俱乐部讲解如果天气突然变坏，水手和游泳者可能会遇到的各种问题。

sentence.content: We also have a lot of volunteers who organise activities to raise money for us, and we couldn’t manage without them.
sentence.content_zh: 我们还有很多志愿者，他们组织活动为机构筹款，没有他们，我们将无法开展工作。

sentence.content: The training we get is a continuous process, focusing on technical competence and safe handling techniques, and it’s given me the confidence to deal with extreme situations without panicking.
sentence.content_zh: 我们会接受持续性的培训，重点是技术能力和安全操作技巧，这让我有信心在处理极端情况时不会惊慌失措。

sentence.content: I was glad I’d done a first aid course before I started, as that’s a big help with the casualty care activities we do.
sentence.content_zh: 我很高兴自己在加入志愿者团队前上了急救课程，因为这对我们开展伤员救护活动大有帮助。

sentence.content: We’ve done a lot on how to deal with ropes and tie knots –that’s an essential skill.
sentence.content_zh: 我们做了很多处理绳索和打绳结的练习-这是一项基本技能。

sentence.content: After a year, I did a one-week residential course, led by specialists.
sentence.content_zh: 一年后，我参加了由专家主持的为期一周的住宿式培训课程。

sentence.content: They had a wave-tank where they could create extreme weather conditions – so we could get experience at what to do if the boat turned over in a storm at night, for example.
sentence.content_zh: 他们有一个可以制造极端天气条件的造波水池-这样我们就可以获得处理有关事件的经验，比如暴风雨夜船翻了。

sentence.content: Since I started, I’ve had to deal with a range of emergency situations. But the work’s hugely motivating.
sentence.content_zh: 自从我开始加入志愿者团队以来，我不得不处理各种紧急情况。但这项工作极大地激励了我。

sentence.content: It’s not just about saving lives – I’ve learned a lot about the technology involved.
sentence.content_zh: 这不仅仅事关拯救生命，我还学到了很多相关技术。

sentence.content: My background in IT’s been useful here, and I can use my expertise to help other volunteers.
sentence.content_zh: 我在信息技术领域的知识背景在这里很有用，我可以用我的专业知识帮助其他志愿者。

sentence.content: They’re a great group – we’re like a family really, which helps when you’re dragging yourself out of bed on a cold stormy night.
sentence.content_zh: 他们是一个很棒的团队-我们就像一个大家庭，这对我们来说很有帮助，比如说当你得在寒冷的暴风雨夜从床上爬起来。

sentence.content: But actually, it’s the colder months that can be the most rewarding time. That’s when the incidents tend to be more serious, and you realise that you can make a huge difference to the outcome.
sentence.content_zh: 但实际上，寒冷的月份才是成就感最大的时候。这时候的事故往往更加严重，你会意识到你可以对结果产生巨大的影响。

sentence.content: So if any of you listeners are interested. . .
sentence.content_zh: 因此，如果各位听众有兴趣……

obj.content: Choose [b] TWO [/b] letters, [b]A–E[/b].
obj.html_content: Choose TWO letters, A–E.

obj.content: Which [b]TWO[/b]  things does David say about the lifeboat volunteer training?
obj.html_content: Which TWO things does David say about the lifeboat volunteer training?

obj.content: The residential course developed his leadership skills.
obj.html_content: The residential course developed his leadership skills.

obj.content: The training in use of ropes and knots was quite brief.
obj.html_content: The training in use of ropes and knots was quite brief.

obj.content: The training exercises have built up his mental strength.
obj.html_content: The training exercises have built up his mental strength.

obj.content: The casualty care activities were particularly challenging for him.
obj.html_content: The casualty care activities were particularly challenging for him.

obj.content: The wave tank activities provided practice in survival techniques.
obj.html_content: The wave tank activities provided practice in survival techniques.

obj.content: working as part of a team
obj.html_content: working as part of a team

sentence.content_zh: 但即使在最恶劣的天气下，也很少有不下水的情况。

sentence.content: As well as going out on the lifeboat, my work involves other things too.
sentence.content_zh: 除了出海救生，我的工作还包括其他方面。

sentence.content: A lot of people underestimate how quickly conditions can change at sea, so I speak to youth groups and sailing clubs in the area about the sorts of problems that sailors and swimmers can have if the weather suddenly gets bad.
sentence.content_zh: 很多人低估了海上条件变化的速度。所以我向该地区的青年团体和帆船俱乐部讲解如果天气突然变坏，水手和游泳者可能会遇到的各种问题。

sentence.content: We also have a lot of volunteers who organise activities to raise money for us, and we couldn’t manage without them.
sentence.content_zh: 我们还有很多志愿者，他们组织活动为机构筹款，没有他们，我们将无法开展工作。

sentence.content: The training we get is a continuous process, focusing on technical competence and safe handling techniques, and it’s given me the confidence to deal with extreme situations without panicking.
sentence.content_zh: 我们会接受持续性的培训，重点是技术能力和安全操作技巧，这让我有信心在处理极端情况时不会惊慌失措。

sentence.content: I was glad I’d done a first aid course before I started, as that’s a big help with the casualty care activities we do.
sentence.content_zh: 我很高兴自己在加入志愿者团队前上了急救课程，因为这对我们开展伤员救护活动大有帮助。

sentence.content: We’ve done a lot on how to deal with ropes and tie knots –that’s an essential skill.
sentence.content_zh: 我们做了很多处理绳索和打绳结的练习-这是一项基本技能。

sentence.content: After a year, I did a one-week residential course, led by specialists.
sentence.content_zh: 一年后，我参加了由专家主持的为期一周的住宿式培训课程。

sentence.content: They had a wave-tank where they could create extreme weather conditions – so we could get experience at what to do if the boat turned over in a storm at night, for example.
sentence.content_zh: 他们有一个可以制造极端天气条件的造波水池-这样我们就可以获得处理有关事件的经验，比如暴风雨夜船翻了。

sentence.content: Since I started, I’ve had to deal with a range of emergency situations. But the work’s hugely motivating.
sentence.content_zh: 自从我开始加入志愿者团队以来，我不得不处理各种紧急情况。但这项工作极大地激励了我。

sentence.content: It’s not just about saving lives – I’ve learned a lot about the technology involved.
sentence.content_zh: 这不仅仅事关拯救生命，我还学到了很多相关技术。

sentence.content: My background in IT’s been useful here, and I can use my expertise to help other volunteers.
sentence.content_zh: 我在信息技术领域的知识背景在这里很有用，我可以用我的专业知识帮助其他志愿者。

sentence.content: They’re a great group – we’re like a family really, which helps when you’re dragging yourself out of bed on a cold stormy night.
sentence.content_zh: 他们是一个很棒的团队-我们就像一个大家庭，这对我们来说很有帮助，比如说当你得在寒冷的暴风雨夜从床上爬起来。

sentence.content: But actually, it’s the colder months that can be the most rewarding time. That’s when the incidents tend to be more serious, and you realise that you can make a huge difference to the outcome.
sentence.content_zh: 但实际上，寒冷的月份才是成就感最大的时候。这时候的事故往往更加严重，你会意识到你可以对结果产生巨大的影响。

sentence.content: So if any of you listeners are interested. . .
sentence.content_zh: 因此，如果各位听众有兴趣……

officialExplain.explain: 题干要求选出David离开伦敦搬到Northsea的原因。原文中，David首先提到自己working in London as a website designer，但随后说到that was interesting，可否定C选项。随后提到自己从小喜欢船，如果搬到海边，能够pursue that interest a bit more in my free time。A选项中的hobby为interest的同义替换，该选项正确。B选项意为“想要缩短工作时长”，在原文中没有提及。

题干要求选出救生艇协会建造的资金来源。题干中的money在原文中替换为funds，后文出现a generous member of the public who lived here all her life，替换了B选项的local resident，需要考生对原文长句有充分理解并且识别替换。A选项的local organisation原文没有提及。C选项的local council在原文中出现了近义词government，但rather than提示并非政府出资，并且此处描述的是协会目前运作的情况，并非建造时的资金来源，和题干要求也不符。

题干中的health assessment原词重现。原文首先出现了B选项的short-sighted，但used to说明目前已没有这个问题，随后的I’d had laser eye surgery也可提示排除B选项。同时，此句也出现了C选项的关键词eye surgery，但原文并没有提到这是医生关注的事情，故排除C选项。下一句中的colour blindness对应A选项，they thought I might have a problem here对应题干中的the doctor was concerned about，因此可确定答案为A选项。

题干问的是志愿者到达救生艇码头后的目标是在多长时间内放船下水。原文首先出现A选项的five minutes，但此处为到达lifeboat station的时间，和题干不符。下一句中try to get the boat off the dock and out to sea替换了题干中的aim to launch the boat，需要考生理解launch此处的含义“使（船）下水”，并识别原文替换。随后出现答案six to eight minutes，对应B选项。下一句的eight and a half minutes为该国的平均时长，与题干要求不符。

本题需选出David负责决定什么事情。原文首先出现B选项原词equipment，但原文为check that the equipment we use is in working order，即“检查设备是否正常运作”，而不是B选项中的“检查需要带哪些设备”。下一句中出现A选项原词crew，但原文为描述crew有特殊的救生衣，并非说话人要决定带哪些船员。最后出现it’s ultimately my decision whether it’s safe to launch the boat，对应C选项，注意C选项中的if意为“是否”，和原文的whether功能相同，引导宾语从句。此处请参照前文“文本解析”第三句的翻译。

题干中的As well as going out on the lifeboat在原文中重现。原文下一句出现speak to youth groups and sailing clubs，对应A选项的give talks；原文中about后谈到演讲内容是“sorts of problems that…if the weather suddenly gets bad”，对应A选项的safety at sea。接下来一句提到raise money，对应B选项的fundraising，但本句中的“We also have a lot of volunteers…and we couldn’t manage without them”说明筹款活动为志愿者们所做，并非说话人所做，故排除B选项。C选项的“招募新志愿者”并未提及。

本题需要选出David关于救生艇志愿者培训所说的两方面内容。原文首先提到it’s given me the confidence to deal with extreme situations without panicking，对应C选项的have built up his mental strength，该选项为正确答案之一。需要考生理解confidence和without panicking的含义。下一句出现D选项的casualty care activities，但未提及challenging。B选项的ropes和knots原词出现，但原文We’ve done a lot和选项中brief不符。接下来出现A选项的residential course，但原文仅提到专家授课，并未提及leadership。随后出现E选项的wave tank，后文get experience at what to do if the boat turned over in a storm at night即选项中的practice in survival techniques，因此可确定E选项为另一个正确答案。此处原文为具体例子，选项则较为概括抽象，需要考生具备一定的理解能力和概括能力。

本题需要选出David觉得志愿服务最激励人心的两个方面。题干中的motivating原词重现，可以用于定位。David首先提到自己可以用IT行业的专业知识帮助其他志愿者，但选项中并无类似表达。随后提到“They’re a great group – we’re like a family…”，对应A选项。下一句中转折词but后出现强调句it’s the colder months that can be the most rewarding time，提示答案出现。colder month对应B选项的winter，most rewarding对应题目中的most motivating。因此本题的两个正确选项为A和B。C、D、E三项内容在录音中并未提及。
